name: Kotopitta (Chicken Pie)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the chicken and place it in a pot with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil, skimming off any froth as it rises.
2. Add onions and cook for about 1 1/2 hours. Remove the chicken from the stock and let it cool.
3. Cut the meat into thin strips.
4. Strain the stock and onions; reserve 1 cup. Return the remaining stock to heat.
5. Melt 5 tablespoons of butter in a saucepan. Add flour and stir until well combined.
6. Gradually add hot stock while stirring continuously until it thickens. Remove from heat.
7. Add grated Parmesan cheese, beaten eggs, chicken strips, ham, salt, pepper, and nutmeg to the sauce. Mix thoroughly.
8. Butter a pan slightly smaller than the phyllo sheets, brushing each one first with melted butter.
9. Pour the filling into the pan, spreading it evenly. Fold over the extending sides of the phyllo dough to partially cover the filling, brushing with melted butter.
10. Cover with remaining phyllo sheets, brushing each sheet first with butter.
11. Trim the phyllo to fit the pan and pour any remaining butter over the pie.
12. Score through the top layers and sprinkle lightly with water.
13. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 1 hour.
